ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the server */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the server */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment type (e.g., production, development, testing) */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., virtual, physical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component (e.g., core 0 cpu_idle, core 0 cpu_frequency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "component_type" TEXT /* Type of component (e.g., CPU, memory, network) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the component (e.g., percentage, megahertz) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the data belongs to */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component the data belongs to */,
  "start_time" REAL /* Start time of the data collection in seconds */,
  "end_time" REAL /* End time of the data collection in seconds */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the data collection in seconds */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption of the component during the data collection in Joules */,
  "timestamp_start" REAL /* Start timestamp of the data collection */,
  "timestamp_end" REAL /* End timestamp of the data collection */,
  "value" REAL /* Actual value of the performance data */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the performance data */,
  "metadata" TEXT /* Additional metadata for the performance data (e.g., average, minimum, maximum) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., developer, system administrator, manager) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view performance data, edit server settings) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the alert belongs to */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component the alert belongs to */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high energy consumption, system crash) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the alert */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the session belongs to */,
  "start_time" REAL /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" REAL /* End time of the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
